div1.entryFree:
Woollen, made of wool: a w. bagpipe, Merch. IV, 1, 56 (the Lowland bagpipe commonly having the bag or sack covered with woollen cloth of a green colour. Johnson wooden, Capell wawling, Steevens swollen, Collier bollen). w. vassals, Cor. III, 2, 9 (== coarse).
Substantively, == cloth made of wool: I could not endure a husband with a beard on his face: I had rather lie in the w. Ado II, 1, 33 (between the blankets without sheets; a beard affecting the skin, in kissing, as disagreeably).
